skills.prompt: This topic covers Draw polygons for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.8(A)). Polygons are closed 2D shapes with straight sides. Students classify by side count: triangle (3), quadrilateral (4), pentagon (5), hexagon (6), etc. Key concepts include polygon vs. non-polygon criteria and regular vs. irregular types. Vocabulary: polygon, closed, straight sides, triangle, quadrilateral, pentagon, hexagon, regular, irregular. Common misconceptions include thinking circles are polygons and not recognizing irregular forms. Activities should include sorting, naming by counting sides, drawing, and finding polygons in the environment appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, emphasize hands-on exploration with physical shapes, measurement tools, and real-world objects that 7-8-year-olds encounter daily.

skills.skill: Three-dimensional shapes in the real world I
skills.prompt: This topic covers Three-dimensional shapes in the real world I for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.8(B)). Students identify three-dimensional shapes (cubes, rectangular prisms, spheres, cones, cylinders, pyramids) and describe their attributes. Key concepts include faces, edges, vertices, and relating 3D shapes to real objects and their 2D faces. Vocabulary: three-dimensional, solid, face, edge, vertex, cube, rectangular prism, sphere, cone, cylinder, pyramid. Common misconceptions include difficulty counting hidden attributes and confusing 2D/3D terminology. Activities should include examining real objects, building with blocks, and sorting by attributes appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, emphasize hands-on exploration with physical shapes, measurement tools, and real-world objects that 7-8-year-olds encounter daily.

This topic covers Count sides and vertices for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.8(C)). Students identify and count faces (flat surfaces), edges (where faces meet), and vertices (corner points) of 3D shapes. Key concepts include systematic counting, understanding that different shapes have different counts, and comparing shapes by these attributes. Vocabulary: face, edge, vertex/vertices, flat surface, corner, three-dimensional. Common misconceptions include miscounting hidden features and confusing edges with sides. Activities should include handling physical objects, marking attributes, and creating comparison charts appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, emphasize hands-on exploration with physical shapes, measurement tools, and real-world objects that 7-8-year-olds encounter daily.

This topic covers Identify quadrilaterals for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.8(C)). Students classify quadrilaterals by their attributes. The hierarchy: squares are rectangles, rectangles are parallelograms, all are quadrilaterals. Trapezoids have exactly one pair of parallel sides. Vocabulary: quadrilateral, rectangle, square, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezoid, parallel, right angle. Common misconceptions include thinking categories are mutually exclusive. Activities should include sorting, Venn diagrams, and attribute-based identification games appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, emphasize hands-on exploration with physical shapes, measurement tools, and real-world objects that 7-8-year-olds encounter daily. Encourage precise mathematical language when describing shapes, measurements, and spatial relationships. Include opportunities for drawing, building, and measuring.

skills.skill: Measure with different customary units: inches, feet, and yards
skills.prompt: This topic covers Measure with different customary units: inches, feet, and yards for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.9(A)). Students measure using objects or non-standard/standard units. Key concepts include end-to-end placement without gaps or overlaps, counting units, and understanding that different units produce different measurements. Vocabulary: measure, length, unit, end to end, gap, overlap, longer, shorter. Common misconceptions include gaps/overlaps and inconsistent units. Activities should include measuring with various objects and discussing the need for standard units appropriate for ages 7-8. skills.skill: Which customary unit of length is appropriate: inches or feet?
skills.prompt: This topic covers Which customary unit of length is appropriate: inches or feet? for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.9(B)). Students select appropriate measurement units for different situations. Key concepts include understanding relative unit sizes, choosing sensible units (inches for small objects, feet/yards for larger), and understanding both customary and metric systems. Vocabulary: unit, inch, foot, yard, centimeter, meter, customary, metric, appropriate. Common misconceptions include confusing systems and not understanding relative sizes. Activities should include estimating, selecting tools, and discussing practical unit choices appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, emphasize hands-on exploration with physical shapes, measurement tools, and real-world objects that 7-8-year-olds encounter daily.

This topic covers Area for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.9(F)). Area measures the surface a shape covers in square units. Key concepts include tiling with unit squares, using Area = length x width for rectangles, and understanding square units. Vocabulary: area, square unit, cover, tile, length, width, multiply. Common misconceptions include confusing area with perimeter and not using square units. Activities should include tiling, grid counting, and formula application appropriate for ages 7-8.
